_doc: Map lenses. Each preset = a named layer set + initial fit_bounds, mirroring the four Folium HTMLs in docs/maps/. Choosing a preset replaces the active layer set; opening a wiki page after that ADDS the page's needed layers without unloading the preset.

tributaries.label: Tributaries
tributaries.subtitle: Rivers + downstream + operating / under-construction hydro
tributaries.description: The unified Nepal river view: tributaries, Nepal-origin downstream systems, and the 261 hydropower projects that are operating or licensed to build. The 311 survey-only sites stay one toggle away in the layer panel.

geopolitics.label: Geopolitics
geopolitics.subtitle: Where Nepal sits in Gangetic basin geography
geopolitics.description: Basin polygons (Nepal-linked + India comparison), origin/control callouts, downstream impact markers, and India reference rivers turned on by default. Answers: which Gangetic systems route through Nepal, which don't, and how much of each upstream basin lies inside the border. Mirrors docs/maps/nepal_geopolitics_river_influence.html.

power_system.label: Power system
power_system.subtitle: Grid, gateways, priority projects, storage
power_system.description: The reader-facing grid view: major transmission network, grid hubs and substations, cross-border gateways and conservative cross-border links, the priority watchlist, storage shortlist, and the operating/build hydropower cloud. QA trace fragments, topology nodes, and the conceptual corridor spine stay available but off by default.

solar_system.label: Solar system
solar_system.subtitle: GHI zones, plants, tender awards, suitability, floating PV
solar_system.description: Solar evidence layers: coarse GHI zones, strategic suitability bands, operating DoED solar plants, NEA's 960 MW tender-award anchors, floating-PV candidates, storage shortlist, and the grid hubs needed to read interconnection pressure. Approximate geometries are labelled as such.

wiki.label: Wiki minimal
wiki.subtitle: Sparse base · let the page drive the layers
wiki.description: A near-empty starting canvas. Useful when you want the wiki page (and its binding's layers_on) to entirely drive what shows up on the map.
